skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar and talking head videos via inference.sh CLI. Recommended: P-Video-Avatar (fastest, cheapest, built-in TTS). Also: OmniHuman, Fabric, PixVerse. Capabilities: audio-driven avatars, text-to-avatar, lipsync videos, talking head generation, virtual presenters. Use for: AI presenters, explainer videos, virtual influencers, dubbing, marketing videos. Triggers: ai avatar, talking head, lipsync, avatar video, virtual presenter, ai spokesperson, audio driven video, heygen alternative, synthesia alternative, talking avatar, lip sync, video avatar, ai presenter, digital human
skill.summary: Create AI avatar and talking head videos using inference.sh CLI with multiple AI models
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Documentation skill containing CLI usage examples for inference.sh API. Static findings are false positives: 'external_commands' detection triggered by markdown code blocks showing `belt app run` syntax (hardcoded arguments, no injection risk); 'network' detection triggered by documentation URLs to inference.sh domain (no credential exposure). No actual executable code or security vulnerabilities present.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar and Talking Head Videos
content.value_statement: Creating professional AI avatar videos requires combining multiple tools and services. This skill provides CLI commands and workflows for generating talking head videos using inference.sh, enabling AI presenters, explainer videos, and dubbing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Generate talking head videos from portrait images and text scripts using built-in TTS
Create audio-driven avatar videos by lip-syncing existing audio to portrait images
Produce multi-language video dubbing by combining speech-to-text, translation, and TTS
Generate AI portraits for use as avatar source images
Use multiple avatar models including P-Video-Avatar, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and CLI installation
Output quality depends on input portrait photo quality and lighting
Some models require separate speech generation step before avatar creation

use_cases.title: Marketing Product Demos
use_cases.description: Create professional product demo videos with AI presenters. Generate consistent presenter avatars that deliver marketing messages in multiple languages.

use_cases.title: Educational Course Videos
use_cases.description: Produce explainer and tutorial videos featuring AI presenters. Automate video creation for educational content without on-camera filming.

use_cases.title: Video Localization and Dubbing
use_cases.description: Translate and dub existing videos into multiple languages. Transcribe source content, translate text, generate speech, and sync avatar lips to new audio.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Avatar Video from Image and Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-avatar-video skill to create a talking head video. I have a portrait image at [image_url] and want the presenter to say: [script_text]. Use the P-Video-Avatar model with [voice_name] voice.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic avatar video generation from a portrait and text script

prompt_templates.title: Create Avatar from Image and Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an avatar video using the ai-avatar-video skill. Use [image_url] as the portrait and [audio_url] as the speech audio. Which avatar model would work best for this combination?
prompt_templates.scenario: Audio-driven avatar video with pre-recorded speech

prompt_templates.title: Dub Existing Video into New Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me dub a video into [target_language]. I have a video at [video_url]. Walk me through the full workflow using the ai-avatar-video skill and related skills for transcription, translation, and TTS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full video dubbing workflow with multiple tools

prompt_templates.title: Generate Portrait and Create Avatar Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete avatar video workflow. First, generate a professional portrait using P-Image with the prompt: [portrait_description]. Then use that image to create a talking head video with the script: [script_text].
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end workflow generating both portrait and avatar video

output_examples.input: Generate an avatar video from portrait.jpg with the script 'Welcome to our product demo' using the P-Video-Avatar model.
output_examples.output: The skill provides the CLI command to generate a talking head video. Success output includes a video file with the portrait lip-syncing to the generated speech. The video is produced in 720p or 1080p resolution depending on settings.

output_examples.input: Create a multilingual dub workflow for a video at sample.mp4 going from English to Spanish.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a 4-step workflow: transcribe with fast-whisper, translate the text, generate Spanish speech with kokoro-tts, then sync the avatar lips using latentsync.

best_practices: Use high-quality portrait photos that are front-facing with good lighting and neutral backgrounds
Start with P-Video-Avatar for fastest results and lowest cost before exploring other models
Generate audio separately for non-TTS models to ensure timing consistency before avatar generation
anti_patterns: Do not use low-resolution or heavily compressed portrait images as input
Avoid using background audio with significant noise in audio-driven avatar models
Do not expect real-time generation; video processing takes several seconds per output second

faq.question: What is the fastest avatar model?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar is the fastest at approximately 1.83 seconds per second of video output. It is also the cheapest at $0.025 per second.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI called 'belt'. Installation instructions are available in the skill documentation.

faq.question: Which avatar model has built-in text-to-speech?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar and Fabric 1.0 both have built-in TTS with multiple voice options. OmniHuman and PixVerse require separate speech generation.

faq.question: Can I use my own portrait photo?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide any portrait image URL. For best results, use front-facing photos with clear visibility of the face and good lighting.

faq.question: How do I create videos in different languages?
faq.answer: For P-Video-Avatar, set the voice_language parameter. For other models, generate speech in the target language first using a TTS skill, then use that audio with the avatar model.

faq.question: Can I dub an existing video into a new language?
faq.answer: Yes, this requires a multi-step workflow: transcribe the original video, translate the text, generate new speech, then use LatentSync to sync the lips to the new audio.
